DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Motion Guideline

Documents the motion system for a style — named easing curves, recommended durations, and usage guidance. Each item in the motion guideline represents a single named easing definition with its cubic-bezier function (aligned with the W3C Design Tokens Format Module cubicBezier type), recommended duration range, usage context, and optional examples. Motion guidelines capture the structured data that tools need to generate CSS transitions, validate animation implementations, and display interactive easing curve previews.

Source: guidelines/motion.schema.json

3 definitions in this file:

  • motionDuration
  • motionEntry
  • motion

motionDuration

A recommended duration range for an easing curve. Duration values are strings representing time values (e.g., '200ms', '0.3s') or token references (e.g., 'duration-quick'). When both min and max are provided, they define a range. When only one is provided, it serves as a point recommendation. When neither is provided, the description field should explain the guidance (e.g., 'depends on element size').

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
minstringoptionalThe minimum recommended duration as a time value or token reference (e.g., '200ms', '0.3s', 'duration-quick'). When omitted, there is no minimum recommendation.
maxstringoptionalThe maximum recommended duration as a time value or token reference (e.g., '500ms', '0.5s', 'duration-slow'). When omitted, there is no maximum recommendation.
descriptionrichTextoptionalFreeform duration guidance. Use when the recommendation cannot be expressed as a simple min/max range (e.g., 'Depends on element size — use shorter durations for small elements and longer durations for full-screen transitions'). Also useful for supplementary notes when min/max are provided.

References: richText

motionEntry

A single named easing definition in the motion system. Each entry documents a cubic-bezier timing function with its recommended usage and duration. The `function` property uses the same four-number array format as the W3C Design Tokens Format Module cubicBezier type: [P1x, P1y, P2x, P2y] where P1x and P2x are in the range [0, 1] and P1y and P2y can be any real number.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the easing (e.g., 'expressive', 'enter', 'exit', 'lateral', 'bounce', 'linear'). Used for cross-referencing and tooling.
descriptionrichTextrequiredWhat this easing is for, when to use it, and how it affects the user experience. Should explain the visual character of the curve (e.g., 'elements accelerate quickly and decelerate into their final position') and the types of transitions it suits.
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name of the easing (e.g., 'Expressive Ease', 'Enter Ease', 'Exit Ease', 'Bounce Ease').
functionnumber[]optionalThe cubic-bezier control points as a four-number array [P1x, P1y, P2x, P2y], aligned with the W3C Design Tokens Format Module cubicBezier type (§8.6). P1x and P2x MUST be in the range [0, 1]. P1y and P2y can be any real number (values outside [0, 1] produce bounce/overshoot effects). Maps directly to CSS cubic-bezier() and DTCG cubicBezier token values. When omitted, the easing is described qualitatively in the description field or references a token via the `token` property.
Min items: 4
tokenstringoptionalReference to a DTCG cubicBezier or transition token that defines this easing (e.g., 'motion-ease-expressive', 'transition-enter'). When both `function` and `token` are present, `function` is the display value and `token` is the authoritative source.
durationmotionDurationoptionalThe recommended duration range when using this easing. Different easings suit different durations — enter transitions are typically longer (300-500ms) to draw attention, while exit transitions are shorter (200-300ms) to feel responsive.
usagestringoptionalA short, comma-separated list of usage contexts for this easing (e.g., 'entering elements', 'exiting elements', 'moving, pushing', 'lateral navigation', 'micro-interactions'). Provides a scannable summary — use description for detailed guidance.
examplesexample[]optionalVisual or interactive examples showing this easing in action — screen recordings of transitions, interactive curve visualizations, or code snippets demonstrating CSS usage.

References: richText, motionDuration, example

Example

[
  {
    "name": "expressive",
    "displayName": "Expressive Ease",
    "description": "The most commonly used easing, and a reliable default choice when unsure. Suitable for elements that both begin and end within the user's field of view, such as size or position changes. Also appropriate for elements entering or exiting in sync with other visible elements.",
    "function": [
      0.55,
      0,
      0,
      1
    ],
    "token": "motion-ease-expressive",
    "usage": "moving, pushing, resizing",
    "duration": {
      "description": "Depends on element size — use shorter durations for small elements and longer durations for full-screen transitions."
    },
    "examples": [
      {
        "title": "Pin opening/closing animation",
        "presentation": {
          "type": "url",
          "url": "https://storybook.acme.com/?path=/story/motion-expressive--pin-open"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "name": "enter",
    "displayName": "Enter Ease",
    "description": "Used for elements that initially exist out of view and smoothly enter the screen. Also appropriate for elements that snap into position after being released from a drag interaction.",
    "function": [
      0.05,
      0.7,
      0.1,
      1
    ],
    "token": "motion-ease-enter",
    "usage": "entering elements, snap-to-position after drag",
    "duration": {
      "min": "300ms",
      "max": "500ms",
      "description": "Enter transitions use longer durations to help users direct their attention to new elements appearing on screen."
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "exit",
    "displayName": "Exit Ease",
    "description": "Used for elements that begin within the user's field of view and passively disappear from the screen without direct user interaction.",
    "function": [
      0.3,
      0,
      0.8,
      0.15
    ],
    "token": "motion-ease-exit",
    "usage": "passive exiting, dismissal",
    "duration": {
      "min": "200ms",
      "max": "300ms",
      "description": "Exit transitions use shorter durations as they require less user attention."
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "lateral",
    "displayName": "Lateral Ease",
    "description": "Used for smooth horizontal transitions of elements at the same hierarchy level, like swiping between tabs or navigating a carousel. Creates natural movement by starting slowly, accelerating in the middle, and slowing toward the end.",
    "function": [
      0.8,
      0,
      0.2,
      1
    ],
    "token": "motion-ease-lateral",
    "usage": "tab navigation, carousel swiping, horizontal transitions",
    "duration": {
      "min": "400ms",
      "max": "500ms"
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "bounce",
    "displayName": "Bounce Ease",
    "description": "Used for light and fun motion that allows users to interact playfully. The overshoot creates a sense of weightlessness. Commonly used for floating elements like toolbars.",
    "function": [
      0,
      0.4,
      0,
      1.4
    ],
    "token": "motion-ease-bounce",
    "usage": "floating elements, toolbar animations, micro-interactions",
    "duration": {
      "min": "100ms",
      "max": "200ms"
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "linear",
    "displayName": "Linear Ease",
    "description": "Used for properties unrelated to spatial motion, such as opacity and color changes. A background color change on hover or a fade-in/fade-out can happen linearly as it provides direct feedback about state without implying physical movement.",
    "function": [
      0,
      0,
      1,
      1
    ],
    "token": "motion-ease-linear",
    "usage": "opacity changes, color transitions, toggles, checkboxes",
    "duration": {
      "description": "Duration varies by context — use the shortest duration that produces a perceptible transition without feeling abrupt."
    }
  }
]

motion

Documents the motion system for a visual style — the named easing curves, their timing functions, recommended durations, and usage guidance. Each item represents a single easing definition that designers and engineers select from when adding animation or transitions. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display easings in this order. A typical motion guideline lists easings from most commonly used to most specialized (e.g., expressive first, then enter, exit, lateral, bounce, linear). This guideline type is scoped to style entities and complements the scale guideline (which documents duration token progressions) and best-practice guidelines (which document motion rules like reduced-motion handling).

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
type"motion"requiredIdentifies this guideline as a motion specification.
itemsmotionEntry[]requiredThe named easing definitions in the motion system. Each item documents a single easing curve with its cubic-bezier function, recommended duration, and usage guidance. The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display easings in this order.
Min items: 1
descriptionrichTextoptionalAn overview of the motion system — the philosophy behind the easing choices, how duration and easing interact, and any system-wide motion principles not covered by individual easing entries.

References: richText, motionEntry

Example

{
  "type": "motion",
  "description": "The motion system defines how animated transitions behave across the product. Easing curves control the acceleration profile of animations, making movement feel natural and responsive. Duration is chosen based on transition size (smaller areas get shorter durations) and direction (enter transitions are longer than exit transitions to draw attention).",
  "items": [
    {
      "name": "expressive",
      "displayName": "Expressive Ease",
      "description": "The default easing for most transitions. Suitable for elements that change size or position within the viewport.",
      "function": [
        0.55,
        0,
        0,
        1
      ],
      "token": "motion-ease-expressive",
      "usage": "moving, pushing, resizing",
      "duration": {
        "description": "Depends on element size."
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "enter",
      "displayName": "Enter Ease",
      "description": "For elements entering the viewport from off-screen or snapping into position after a drag.",
      "function": [
        0.05,
        0.7,
        0.1,
        1
      ],
      "token": "motion-ease-enter",
      "usage": "entering elements",
      "duration": {
        "min": "300ms",
        "max": "500ms"
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "exit",
      "displayName": "Exit Ease",
      "description": "For elements passively leaving the viewport without direct user interaction.",
      "function": [
        0.3,
        0,
        0.8,
        0.15
      ],
      "token": "motion-ease-exit",
      "usage": "exiting elements",
      "duration": {
        "min": "200ms",
        "max": "300ms"
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "lateral",
      "displayName": "Lateral Ease",
      "description": "For horizontal transitions between peers at the same hierarchy level.",
      "function": [
        0.8,
        0,
        0.2,
        1
      ],
      "token": "motion-ease-lateral",
      "usage": "tab navigation, carousels",
      "duration": {
        "min": "400ms",
        "max": "500ms"
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "bounce",
      "displayName": "Bounce Ease",
      "description": "For playful micro-interactions on floating elements like toolbars.",
      "function": [
        0,
        0.4,
        0,
        1.4
      ],
      "token": "motion-ease-bounce",
      "usage": "floating elements, micro-interactions",
      "duration": {
        "min": "100ms",
        "max": "200ms"
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "linear",
      "displayName": "Linear Ease",
      "description": "For non-spatial property changes like opacity and color.",
      "function": [
        0,
        0,
        1,
        1
      ],
      "token": "motion-ease-linear",
      "usage": "opacity, color transitions"
    }
  ]
}
